message.content: Adversaries may exploit GUI input capture to deceive users into providing credentials by mimicking legitimate operating system prompts. This technique involves creating fake dialog boxes or forms that appear authentic, such as those requesting elevated privileges or login information. Commonly used in both Windows and macOS environments, adversaries employ various scripting languages like AppleScript, PowerShell, and Unix Shell to generate these prompts. Notable malware families such as Bundlore, Calisto, Dok, FIN4, iKitten, Keydnap, Metamorfo, Proton, and SILENTTRINITY have utilized this method to harvest credentials. The impact of this technique can be severe, leading to unauthorized access and potential data breaches. Mitigation strategies include user education to recognize phishing attempts, implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) to reduce reliance on passwords, and employing endpoint security solutions that can detect and block malicious scripts and prompts. Regular system updates and patches can also help prevent exploitation by addressing vulnerabilities that adversaries might leverage.
